title: Take your medicine: the punch-out
microFact: From the trees, the pros' default is a sideways punch back to the fairway. One wasted shot beats three.
lesson:
You will hit it into the trees. What happens NEXT decides your score.

The amateur move: spotting a 2-meter gap in the branches and attempting the miracle 160m draw. Result, four times out of five: ricochet, still in the trees, now angry. The hole ends in a 9.

The player's move: TAKE YOUR MEDICINE. Find the widest, safest exit — even if it's sideways or backwards — punch out to the fairway, and play the hole from there. Bogey or double, controlled.

The PUNCH-OUT technique (worth one range session): ball back in your stance, a 5- or 6-iron, hands ahead, abbreviated swing — a firm putt-like chop that keeps the ball under branches and running.

The decision rule: attempt a recovery ONLY if you'd bet ₹500 on pulling it off at even odds. Be honest. Under that bar, punch out — no debate, no lingering.

This is also a belonging signal, oddly enough: experienced partners watch a newcomer in trouble. Chip out sideways without drama and someone will say "good golf" — and mean it.

quiz.question: Deep in trees, one risky gap toward the green, easy sideways exit to the fairway. The disciplined play?
options: Punch out sideways to the fairway
Hit it low through the gap with a putter
Thread the gap — fortune favors the brave
quiz.explanation: Guaranteed recovery beats a low-percentage hero shot. One deliberate 'wasted' stroke caps the damage at bogey territory.
